page_content:
LARC452 Green Infrastructure and Community Greening (3 Credits) A critical look and exploration of green infrastructure (GI) elements in the built environment in contributing to ecosystems services and the sustainability of the built environment. The course explores the science, issues, challenges, and the policy, planning and design solutions offered by green infrastructure. Prerequisite: PLSC110 and PLSC111; or (PLSC112 and PLSC113); or permission of instructor. Restriction: Junior standing or higher. Credit Only Granted for: LARC489G or LARC452. Formerly: LARC489G.

LARC413 Construction Technology III: Water Soil-Centric Practices (4 Credits) Third of three courses in the landscape architecture engineering and construction sequence focuses on site construction, soil and water- centric Best Design Practices (BDPs). Included in this subject area are the topics of construction operations and earthwork, soil erosion and sediment control, retaining wall, soil and constructed media, shoreline construction, bioretention and planting systems and green roofs. Prerequisite: LARC412. Restriction: Must be in Landscape Architecture program.
